channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: ViGText: Deepfake Image Detection with Vision-Language Model Explanations and Graph Neural Networks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes ViGText, a multimodal deepfake detection method that fuses image patches with Vision LLM (VLLM) textual explanations into graph representations processed by Graph Neural Networks.
  • Employs multi-level spatial and frequency feature extraction and constructs separate image and text graphs which are integrated to improve detection generalization and robustness.
  • Reports large performance gains (e.g., average F1 from 72.45% to 98.32%, +11.1% recall) and shows resistance to targeted attacks on its graph architecture (performance drop <4%).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ahmad ALBarqawi', 'Mahmoud Nazzal', 'Issa Khalil', 'Abdallah Khreishah', 'NhatHai Phan']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'vision-language models', 'graph neural networks', 'robustness', 'multimodal security']

item.title: CityGuard: Graph-Aware Private Descriptors for Bias-Resilient Identity Search Across Urban Cameras
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces CityGuard, a topology-aware transformer for privacy-preserving person re-identification across distributed urban cameras.
  • Combines three components: a dispersion-adaptive metric learner for tighter intra-class features, spatially conditioned graph self-attention for coarse geometry-aware cross-view alignment, and differentially private embedding maps with compact indexes for secure decentralized retrieval.
  • Claims tunable privacy-utility trade-offs under rigorous differential-privacy accounting and demonstrates improved retrieval precision and throughput on standard benchmarks and large-scale retrieval studies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rong Fu', 'Wenxin Zhang', 'Yibo Meng', 'Jia Yee Tan', 'Jiaxuan Lu', 'Rui Lu', 'Jiekai Wu', 'Zhaolu Kang', 'Simon Fong']
  • Tags: ['differential privacy', 'privacy-preserving embeddings', 'person re-identification / surveillance', 'robustness / defenses', 'graph-aware transformer']

item.title: Jailbreaking Leaves a Trace: Understanding and Detecting Jailbreak Attacks from Internal Representations of Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes internal hidden activations of multiple LLMs (GPT-J, LLaMA, Mistral, Mamba) to identify consistent latent-space signatures that distinguish jailbreak prompts from benign prompts.
  • Proposes a tensor-based latent representation framework for lightweight jailbreak detection that requires no model fine-tuning or external LLM detectors.
  • Demonstrates an inference-time intervention that selectively bypasses high-susceptibility layers to block jailbreaks (78% blocked) while largely preserving benign behavior (94% preserved) on an ablated LLaMA-3.1-8B.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sri Durga Sai Sowmya Kadali', 'Evangelos E. Papalexakis']
  • Tags: ['jailbreak', 'jailbreak-detection', 'latent-representations', 'inference-time-defense', 'adversarial-prompts']

item.title: A False Sense of Privacy: Evaluating Textual Data Sanitization Beyond Surface-level Privacy Leakage
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a framework to evaluate re-identification attacks that reveal semantic-level privacy leakage beyond explicit PII removal.
  • Demonstrates that auxiliary contextual cues in sanitized text can be used to infer sensitive attributes (e.g., age, substance use), and that commercial PII removal (e.g., Azure) can fail substantially (74% leakage on MedQA).
  • Assesses differential privacy as a mitigation: it reduces risk but substantially degrades utility, motivating the need for more robust semantic-level sanitization methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rui Xin', 'Niloofar Mireshghallah', 'Shuyue Stella Li', 'Michael Duan', 'Hyunwoo Kim', 'Yejin Choi', 'Yulia Tsvetkov', 'Sewoong Oh', 'Pang Wei Koh']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 're-identification', 'text sanitization', 'PII removal', 'differential privacy']

item.title: TFL: Targeted Bit-Flip Attack on Large Language Model
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TFL, a targeted bit-flip attack framework that manipulates LLM outputs for specific prompts while minimizing impact on unrelated inputs.
  • Proposes a keyword-focused attack loss to promote attacker-specified target tokens and an auxiliary utility score to balance attack success against collateral degradation.
  • Demonstrates effectiveness on multiple LLMs (Qwen, DeepSeek, Llama) and benchmarks (DROP, GSM8K, TriviaQA), achieving targeted manipulations with fewer than 50 bit flips.
  • Shows substantially reduced effect on benign queries compared to prior (un-targeted) bit-flip attacks, highlighting a stealthy, precise model-integrity threat.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jingkai Guo', 'Chaitali Chakrabarti', 'Deliang Fan']
  • Tags: ['bit-flip attack', 'model fault injection', 'LLM security', 'targeted model attack']

item.title: Reducing Text Bias in Synthetically Generated MCQAs for VLMs in Autonomous Driving
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies that synthetically generated multiple-choice QA (MCQA) for driving tasks contain textual cues that let VLMs achieve high accuracy without using visual input.
  • Demonstrates a VLM fine-tuned on such biased synthetic data attains human-like accuracy even when blind to images, revealing a major evaluation vulnerability.
  • Proposes a method that decouples correct answers from linguistic artifacts and uses a curriculum learning strategy to force visual grounding.
  • Shows reduction of blind accuracy advantage from +66.9% above random to +2.9%, largely eliminating exploitable textual shortcuts.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sutej Kulgod', 'Sean Ye', 'Sanchit Tanwar', 'Christoffer Heckman']
  • Tags: ['dataset bias', 'robustness', 'vision-language models', 'evaluation integrity', 'synthetic data']

item.title: The Oversight Game: Learning to Cooperatively Balance an AI Agent's Safety and Autonomy
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a minimal oversight control interface where an agent chooses to act autonomously or defer (ask) while a human chooses to trust or oversee, formalized as a two-player Markov game.
  • Proves an alignment guarantee for Markov Potential Games: increases in agent autonomy that raise the agent's utility cannot reduce the human's value, creating intrinsic coupling of agent autonomy and human welfare.
  • Validates the framework in gridworld simulations and an agentic tool-use task by fine-tuning two 30B parameter language models with policy gradients, showing reduced safety violations and emergent cooperative oversight behavior.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['William Overman', 'Mohsen Bayati']
  • Tags: ['human-in-the-loop', 'oversight', 'alignment', 'safety mechanisms', 'agent autonomy']

item.title: On the Adversarial Robustness of Learning-based Conformal Novelty Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes adversarial robustness of conformal novelty detection, focusing on AdaDetect and a one-class classifier conformal approach that provide finite-sample FDR control.
  • Develops an oracle worst-case attack formulation that upper-bounds the degradation of FDR and motivates a practical, query-only black-box attack scheme.
  • Implements and evaluates two complementary black-box adversarial algorithms on synthetic and real-world datasets, showing attacks can substantially increase FDR while preserving detection power.
  • Highlights fundamental vulnerabilities of current error-controlled novelty detection methods and motivates the need for more robust defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Daofu Zhang', 'Mehrdad Pournaderi', 'Hanne M. Clifford', 'Yu Xiang', 'Pramod K. Varshney']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-robustness', 'novelty-detection', 'adversarial-attacks', 'conformal-prediction', 'black-box-attacks']

item.title: ViGText: Deepfake Image Detection with Vision-Language Model Explanations and Graph Neural Networks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes ViGText, a multimodal deepfake detection method that fuses image patches with Vision LLM (VLLM) textual explanations into graph representations processed by Graph Neural Networks.
  • Employs multi-level spatial and frequency feature extraction and constructs separate image and text graphs which are integrated to improve detection generalization and robustness.
  • Reports large performance gains (e.g., average F1 from 72.45% to 98.32%, +11.1% recall) and shows resistance to targeted attacks on its graph architecture (performance drop <4%).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ahmad ALBarqawi', 'Mahmoud Nazzal', 'Issa Khalil', 'Abdallah Khreishah', 'NhatHai Phan']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'vision-language models', 'graph neural networks', 'robustness', 'multimodal security']

item.title: A False Sense of Privacy: Evaluating Textual Data Sanitization Beyond Surface-level Privacy Leakage
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a framework to evaluate re-identification attacks that reveal semantic-level privacy leakage beyond explicit PII removal.
  • Demonstrates that auxiliary contextual cues in sanitized text can be used to infer sensitive attributes (e.g., age, substance use), and that commercial PII removal (e.g., Azure) can fail substantially (74% leakage on MedQA).
  • Assesses differential privacy as a mitigation: it reduces risk but substantially degrades utility, motivating the need for more robust semantic-level sanitization methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rui Xin', 'Niloofar Mireshghallah', 'Shuyue Stella Li', 'Michael Duan', 'Hyunwoo Kim', 'Yejin Choi', 'Yulia Tsvetkov', 'Sewoong Oh', 'Pang Wei Koh']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 're-identification', 'text sanitization', 'PII removal', 'differential privacy']

item.title: Communication-Corruption Coupling and Verification in Cooperative Multi-Objective Bandits
item.description:
Summary

  • Studies cooperative multi-agent stochastic multi-armed bandits under adversarial corruption of feedback with a global corruption budget Γ and limited verification.
  • Introduces a communication-corruption coupling showing how protocol/communication choices amplify or preserve effective corruption (from Γ up to NΓ) via a multiplicity functional, with regret bounds parameterized by this effective corruption.
  • Provides information-theoretic lower bounds (unavoidable Ω(Γ) penalty and a high-corruption regime where sublinear regret is impossible) and shows how a budget ν of verified observations can restore learnability once past a threshold.
  • Analyzes defenses via verification/certified sharing that can make team regret independent of Γ, and compares raw-sample, summary, and recommendation-only sharing regimes.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ming Shi']
  • Tags: ['adversarial corruption', 'robustness', 'multi-agent bandits', 'verification', 'distributed learning']

item.title: M3OOD: Automatic Selection of Multimodal OOD Detectors
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces M3OOD, a meta-learning framework that recommends the best OOD detector for a given multimodal distribution shift by leveraging historical detector performance.
  • Represents datasets using multimodal embeddings plus handcrafted meta-features capturing distributional and cross-modal characteristics to predict detector performance.
  • Demonstrates that M3OOD outperforms 10 baselines across 12 test scenarios with low computational overhead.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuehan Qin', 'Li Li', 'Defu Cao', 'Tiankai Yang', 'Jiate Li', 'Yue Zhao']
  • Tags: ['OOD detection', 'meta-learning', 'robustness', 'model selection', 'multimodal']

item.title: How Well Can Differential Privacy Be Audited in One Run?
item.description:
Summary

  • Analyzes the limits of one-run auditing for differential privacy, quantifying how precisely a single training run can lower-bound an algorithm's true privacy parameter.
  • Identifies interference between observable effects of different data elements as the primary barrier to one-run auditing efficacy.
  • Proposes new conceptual approaches to reduce such interference and thereby improve practical performance of one-run auditing for real ML algorithms.
  • Builds on Steinke et al. (2024) and bridges theoretical characterization with practical considerations for auditing DP in a single run.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Amit Keinan', 'Moshe Shenfeld', 'Katrina Ligett']
  • Tags: ['differential_privacy', 'privacy_auditing', 'membership_privacy', 'auditing_methods', 'theoretical_analysis']

item.title: Expressiveness of Multi-Neuron Convex Relaxations in Neural Network Certification
item.description:
Summary

  • Proves that multi-neuron convex relaxations are inherently incomplete for certifying ReLU networks, extending the known single-neuron convex barrier to a universal convex barrier.
  • Shows positive results: completeness can be recovered by either (i) augmenting the network with a polynomial number of carefully designed ReLU neurons, or (ii) partitioning the input domain into convex sub-polytopes.
  • Demonstrates that multi-neuron relaxations have theoretical capabilities beyond single-neuron relaxations (e.g., better partition complexity and realizability), and outlines implications for certified robustness methods and verification algorithms.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuhao Mao', 'Yani Zhang', 'Martin Vechev']
  • Tags: ['certified robustness', 'formal verification', 'convex relaxation', 'adversarial robustness', 'verification theory']

item.title: CityGuard: Graph-Aware Private Descriptors for Bias-Resilient Identity Search Across Urban Cameras
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces CityGuard, a topology-aware transformer for privacy-preserving person re-identification across distributed urban cameras.
  • Combines three components: a dispersion-adaptive metric learner for tighter intra-class features, spatially conditioned graph self-attention for coarse geometry-aware cross-view alignment, and differentially private embedding maps with compact indexes for secure decentralized retrieval.
  • Claims tunable privacy-utility trade-offs under rigorous differential-privacy accounting and demonstrates improved retrieval precision and throughput on standard benchmarks and large-scale retrieval studies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rong Fu', 'Wenxin Zhang', 'Yibo Meng', 'Jia Yee Tan', 'Jiaxuan Lu', 'Rui Lu', 'Jiekai Wu', 'Zhaolu Kang', 'Simon Fong']
  • Tags: ['differential privacy', 'privacy-preserving embeddings', 'person re-identification / surveillance', 'robustness / defenses', 'graph-aware transformer']

item.title: TFL: Targeted Bit-Flip Attack on Large Language Model
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TFL, a targeted bit-flip attack framework that manipulates LLM outputs for specific prompts while minimizing impact on unrelated inputs.
  • Proposes a keyword-focused attack loss to promote attacker-specified target tokens and an auxiliary utility score to balance attack success against collateral degradation.
  • Demonstrates effectiveness on multiple LLMs (Qwen, DeepSeek, Llama) and benchmarks (DROP, GSM8K, TriviaQA), achieving targeted manipulations with fewer than 50 bit flips.
  • Shows substantially reduced effect on benign queries compared to prior (un-targeted) bit-flip attacks, highlighting a stealthy, precise model-integrity threat.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jingkai Guo', 'Chaitali Chakrabarti', 'Deliang Fan']
  • Tags: ['bit-flip attack', 'model fault injection', 'LLM security', 'targeted model attack']

item.title: Trojans in Artificial Intelligence (TrojAI) Final Report
item.description:
Summary

  • Programmatic synthesis of IARPA's TrojAI initiative: maps the threat of AI Trojans (hidden backdoors) and catalogs their characteristics and risks.
  • Describes and evaluates detection methodologies such as weight analysis and trigger inversion, with comprehensive test/evaluation results on detector performance and sensitivity.
  • Presents mitigation approaches for reducing Trojan risks in deployed models and discusses prevalence of naturally occurring (unintentional) Trojans, open challenges, and research recommendations.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kristopher W. Reese', 'Taylor Kulp-McDowall', 'Michael Majurski', 'Tim Blattner', 'Derek Juba', 'Peter Bajcsy', 'Antonio Cardone', 'Philippe Dessauw', 'Alden Dima', 'Anthony J. Kearsley', 'Melinda Kleczynski', 'Joel Vasanth', 'Walid Keyrouz', 'Chace Ashcraft', 'Neil Fendley', 'Ted Staley', 'Trevor Stout', 'Josh Carney', 'Greg Canal', 'Will Redman', 'Aurora Schmidt', 'Cameron Hickert', 'William Paul', 'Jared Markowitz', 'Nathan Drenkow', 'David Shriver', 'Marissa Connor', 'Keltin Grimes', 'Marco Christiani', 'Hayden Moore', 'Jordan Widjaja', 'Kasimir Gabert', 'Uma Balakrishnan', 'Satyanadh Gundimada', 'John Jacobellis', 'Sandya Lakkur', 'Vitus Leung', 'Jon Roose', 'Casey Battaglino', 'Farinaz Koushanfar', 'Greg Fields', 'Xihe Gu', 'Yaman Jandali', 'Xinqiao Zhang', 'Akash Vartak', 'Tim Oates', 'Ben Erichson', 'Michael Mahoney', 'Rauf Izmailov', 'Xiangyu Zhang', 'Guangyu Shen', 'Siyuan Cheng', 'Shiqing Ma', 'XiaoFeng Wang', 'Haixu Tang', 'Di Tang', 'Xiaoyi Chen', 'Zihao Wang', 'Rui Zhu', 'Susmit Jha', 'Xiao Lin', 'Manoj Acharya', 'Wenchao Li', 'Chao Chen']
  • Tags: ['backdoor/trojans', 'attack detection', 'trigger inversion', 'model inspection/weight analysis', 'mitigation/defense']

item.title: PRISM-FCP: Byzantine-Resilient Federated Conformal Prediction via Partial Sharing
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces PRISM-FCP, a federated conformal prediction framework that is resilient to Byzantine (malicious) clients by using partial parameter sharing during training and robust aggregation during calibration.
  • During training, clients transmit only M of D parameters per round, reducing the expected energy of adversarial perturbations by factor M/D and yielding lower MSE and tighter prediction intervals.
  • During calibration, nonconformity scores are converted to characterization vectors and distance-based maliciousness scores are used to downweight or filter suspected Byzantine contributions before estimating the conformal quantile.
  • Empirical results on synthetic data and the UCI Superconductivity dataset show PRISM-FCP maintains nominal coverage under Byzantine attacks while avoiding interval inflation and reducing communication.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ehsan Lari', 'Reza Arablouei', 'Stefan Werner']
  • Tags: ['federated learning', 'Byzantine resilience', 'model poisoning defense', 'conformal prediction', 'robust aggregation']

item.title: Generating adversarial inputs for a graph neural network model of AC power flow
item.description:
Summary

  • Formulates optimization problems to generate adversarial input points that cause large errors between a GNN surrogate's predicted AC power flow and true AC power flow solutions.
  • Demonstrates attacks on the CANOS-PF (PFΔ) graph neural network on a 14-bus test grid, producing errors up to 3.4 p.u. in reactive power and 0.08 p.u. in voltage magnitude.
  • Shows minimal perturbations (as small as 0.04 p.u. on a single bus) can satisfy adversarial constraints, and motivates development of verification and robust training methods for power-flow surrogates.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Robert Parker']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'graph-neural-networks', 'power-systems', 'robustness', 'verification']

item.title: A Geometric Probe of the Accuracy-Robustness Trade-off: Sharp Boundaries in Symmetry-Breaking Dimensional Expansion
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Symmetry-Breaking Dimensional Expansion (SBDE): inserting constant-valued auxiliary pixels to probe geometry of accuracy vs. adversarial robustness.
  • Shows SBDE consistently increases clean accuracy (e.g., CIFAR-10 ResNet-18) but markedly reduces robustness to iterative white-box attacks.
  • Demonstrates a test-time mask projection (resetting auxiliary pixels to training values) largely neutralizes attacks, attributing vulnerability to sharp loss gradients along the inserted dimensions.
  • Provides a geometric explanation: optimization deepens accuracy basins while creating steep boundaries along auxiliary axes, producing fragile off-manifold sensitivity.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yu Bai', 'Zhe Wang', 'Jiarui Zhang', 'Dong-Xiao Zhang', 'Yinjun Gao', 'Jun-Jie Zhang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'robustness', 'defense', 'geometric analysis']

item.title: Distribution-Free Sequential Prediction with Abstentions
item.description:
Summary

  • Studies a semi-adversarial sequential prediction model where an adversary can inject arbitrarily many corrupted instances into a stream of i.i.d. samples, while the learner may abstain on rounds (no penalty if the instance was corrupted).
  • Proposes AbstainBoost, a boosting-based algorithm that achieves distribution-free learning (unknown clean distribution) with sublinear error for general VC classes against oblivious adversaries.
  • Provides extensions/guarantees for adaptive adversaries on structured function classes (e.g., linear classifiers) and proves lower bounds showing a polynomial trade-off between misclassification error and erroneous abstentions.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jialin Yu', 'Mo\\"ise Blanchard']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-robustness', 'abstention', 'online-learning', 'boosting', 'theoretical-ml']

item.title: JAX-Privacy: A library for differentially private machine learning
item.description:
Summary

  • JAX-Privacy is a software library to facilitate differentially private machine learning, emphasizing usability, flexibility, and efficiency.
  • Provides verified, modular primitives for DP mechanisms including batch selection, gradient clipping, noise addition, privacy accounting, and auditing.
  • Targets both researchers (deep customization) and practitioners (out-of-the-box use), consolidating recent DP-ML research into reusable components.

Paper Type

  • Other

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ryan McKenna', 'Galen Andrew', 'Borja Balle', 'Vadym Doroshenko', 'Arun Ganesh', 'Weiwei Kong', 'Alex Kurakin', 'Brendan McMahan', 'Mikhail Pravilov']
  • Tags: ['differential-privacy', 'privacy-preserving-ml', 'dp-sgd', 'privacy-accounting', 'software-library']

item.title: Two Calm Ends and the Wild Middle: A Geometric Picture of Memorization in Diffusion Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Provides a geometric framework partitioning the diffusion noise schedule into three regimes (small, medium, large noise) that govern memorization vs. generalization based on Gaussian shell coverage and posterior concentration.
  • Identifies a 'danger zone' at medium noise levels where memorization risk is highest, while small and large noise regimes resist memorization via different mechanisms.
  • Analyzes how data geometry influences memorization and interaction across noise scales, and proposes a geometry-informed targeted intervention that mitigates memorization in the medium-noise regime.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Nick Dodson', 'Xinyu Gao', 'Qingsong Wang', 'Yusu Wang', 'Zhengchao Wan']
  • Tags: ['diffusion-models', 'memorization', 'privacy', 'defense', 'theory']

item.title: Asking Forever: Universal Activations Behind Turn Amplification in Conversational LLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies a new failure mode called 'turn amplification' where conversational LLMs are adversarially driven to prolong multi-turn interactions by repeatedly issuing clarification-seeking responses instead of completing tasks.
  • Shows a mechanistic cause: a query-independent, universal activation subspace linked to clarification-seeking behavior that persists across prompts and tasks.
  • Demonstrates scalable attack vectors: supply-chain attacks via fine-tuning and runtime attacks via low-level parameter corruptions that shift models toward this behavior.
  • Evaluates defenses and finds existing protections offer limited mitigation against this class of cost-amplifying, compliant attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zachary Coalson', 'Bo Fang', 'Sanghyun Hong']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-attack', 'supply-chain-attack', 'runtime-parameter-corruption', 'activation-subspace', 'cost-amplification']

item.title: Provable Adversarial Robustness in In-Context Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a distributionally robust meta-learning framework to provide worst-case guarantees for in-context learning (ICL) under Wasserstein adversarial distribution shifts.
  • Derives non-asymptotic theoretical bounds for linear self-attention Transformers linking adversarial perturbation strength (ρ), model capacity (m), and number of in-context examples (N).
  • Finds scaling laws: maximum tolerable perturbation scales as ρ_max ∝ √m and sample complexity penalty scales as N_ρ - N_0 ∝ ρ^2.
  • Validates theoretical predictions with experiments on synthetic tasks and discusses implications of model capacity as a resource for distributional robustness.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Di Zhang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'distributional robustness', 'in-context learning', 'theoretical guarantees', 'transformers']

item.title: Can LLM Safety Be Ensured by Constraining Parameter Regions?
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematically evaluates four methods for identifying "safety regions" in model parameter space at varying granularities (individual weights to whole Transformer layers) across four LLM families and sizes.
  • Uses ten safety identification datasets and additional utility (non-harmful) datasets to refine regions and measures overlap using IoU.
  • Finds only low-to-moderate overlap among identified safety regions and a substantial drop in overlap after refinement with utility datasets, indicating instability and dataset-dependence of identified regions.
  • Concludes that current techniques do not reliably identify stable, dataset-agnostic parameter regions that can be constrained to ensure LLM safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zongmin Li', 'Jian Su', 'Farah Benamara', 'Aixin Sun']
  • Tags: ['LLM-safety', 'parameter-space', 'model-editing', 'robustness', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: Agentic Unlearning: When LLM Agent Meets Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces 'agentic unlearning' to remove specified information from both model parameters and persistent agent memory in closed-loop LLM agents.
  • Identifies two gaps: parameter-memory backflow (memory reactivates parametric remnants) and lack of unified unlearning across both pathways.
  • Proposes Synchronized Backflow Unlearning (SBU): dependency-closure-based memory pruning and stochastic reference alignment for parametric suppression, integrated via a synchronized dual-update closed-loop protocol.
  • Evaluated on medical QA benchmarks, showing reduced traces of targeted private information across both pathways with limited utility degradation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bin Wang', 'Fan Wang', 'Pingping Wang', 'Jinyu Cong', 'Yang Yu', 'Yilong Yin', 'Zhongyi Han', 'Benzheng Wei']
  • Tags: ['machine unlearning', 'privacy', 'defense', 'LLM agents', 'data removal']

item.title: Reducing Text Bias in Synthetically Generated MCQAs for VLMs in Autonomous Driving
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies that synthetically generated multiple-choice QA (MCQA) for driving tasks contain textual cues that let VLMs achieve high accuracy without using visual input.
  • Demonstrates a VLM fine-tuned on such biased synthetic data attains human-like accuracy even when blind to images, revealing a major evaluation vulnerability.
  • Proposes a method that decouples correct answers from linguistic artifacts and uses a curriculum learning strategy to force visual grounding.
  • Shows reduction of blind accuracy advantage from +66.9% above random to +2.9%, largely eliminating exploitable textual shortcuts.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sutej Kulgod', 'Sean Ye', 'Sanchit Tanwar', 'Christoffer Heckman']
  • Tags: ['dataset bias', 'robustness', 'vision-language models', 'evaluation integrity', 'synthetic data']

item.title: ViGText: Deepfake Image Detection with Vision-Language Model Explanations and Graph Neural Networks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes ViGText, a multimodal deepfake detection method that fuses image patches with Vision LLM (VLLM) textual explanations into graph representations processed by Graph Neural Networks.
  • Employs multi-level spatial and frequency feature extraction and constructs separate image and text graphs which are integrated to improve detection generalization and robustness.
  • Reports large performance gains (e.g., average F1 from 72.45% to 98.32%, +11.1% recall) and shows resistance to targeted attacks on its graph architecture (performance drop <4%).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ahmad ALBarqawi', 'Mahmoud Nazzal', 'Issa Khalil', 'Abdallah Khreishah', 'NhatHai Phan']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'vision-language models', 'graph neural networks', 'robustness', 'multimodal security']

item.title: TrapFlow: Controllable Website Fingerprinting Defense via Dynamic Backdoor Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TrapFlow, a controllable website-fingerprinting (WF) defense that injects crafted trigger sequences into target website traffic using backdoor-learning techniques.
  • TrapFlow creates a dual-effect: if an attacker trains on poisoned data the model learns the trigger as a dominant feature causing broad misclassification; if the attacker trains only on clean data the trigger acts as an adversarial patch at inference to induce misclassification.
  • Trigger optimization uses a Fast-Levenshtein-like distance to maximize learnability and distinctiveness; experiments show reduction of a strong RF attack from 99% to 6% accuracy with 74% data overhead, outperforming FRONT and Palette baselines.
  • Method is validated in real Tor network environments, demonstrating practical applicability to traffic-analysis privacy defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Siyuan Liang', 'Jiajun Gong', 'Tianmeng Fang', 'Aishan Liu', 'Tao Wang', 'Xiaochun Cao', 'Dacheng Tao', 'Ee-Chien Chang']
  • Tags: ['website-fingerprinting', 'backdoor-learning', 'poisoning/defense', 'adversarial-triggers', 'traffic-analysis/Tor']

item.title: Expressiveness of Multi-Neuron Convex Relaxations in Neural Network Certification
item.description:
Summary

  • Proves that multi-neuron convex relaxations are inherently incomplete for certifying ReLU networks, extending the known single-neuron convex barrier to a universal convex barrier.
  • Shows positive results: completeness can be recovered by either (i) augmenting the network with a polynomial number of carefully designed ReLU neurons, or (ii) partitioning the input domain into convex sub-polytopes.
  • Demonstrates that multi-neuron relaxations have theoretical capabilities beyond single-neuron relaxations (e.g., better partition complexity and realizability), and outlines implications for certified robustness methods and verification algorithms.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuhao Mao', 'Yani Zhang', 'Martin Vechev']
  • Tags: ['certified robustness', 'formal verification', 'convex relaxation', 'adversarial robustness', 'verification theory']

item.title: Adaptive GR(1) Specification Repair for Liveness-Preserving Shielding in Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes an adaptive shielding framework for reinforcement learning using GR(1) (a fragment of LTL) that can detect environment-assumption violations at runtime and repair specifications online.
  • Uses Inductive Logic Programming (ILP) to automatically and interpretablely repair GR(1) specifications so that liveness remains achievable while minimally weakening goals only when necessary.
  • Demonstrates on Minepump and Atari Seaquest that adaptive shields maintain near-optimal auxiliary rewards and perfect logical compliance, outperforming static symbolic controllers and static shields.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tiberiu-Andrei Georgescu', 'Alexander W. Goodall', 'Dalal Alrajeh', 'Francesco Belardinelli', 'Sebastian Uchitel']
  • Tags: ['safety', 'reinforcement learning', 'specification repair', 'runtime shielding', 'formal methods']

item.title: The Oversight Game: Learning to Cooperatively Balance an AI Agent's Safety and Autonomy
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a minimal oversight control interface where an agent chooses to act autonomously or defer (ask) while a human chooses to trust or oversee, formalized as a two-player Markov game.
  • Proves an alignment guarantee for Markov Potential Games: increases in agent autonomy that raise the agent's utility cannot reduce the human's value, creating intrinsic coupling of agent autonomy and human welfare.
  • Validates the framework in gridworld simulations and an agentic tool-use task by fine-tuning two 30B parameter language models with policy gradients, showing reduced safety violations and emergent cooperative oversight behavior.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['William Overman', 'Mohsen Bayati']
  • Tags: ['human-in-the-loop', 'oversight', 'alignment', 'safety mechanisms', 'agent autonomy']

item.title: PenTiDef: Enhancing Privacy and Robustness in Decentralized Federated Intrusion Detection Systems against Poisoning Attacks
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes PenTiDef, a defense framework for decentralized federated intrusion detection systems (DFL-IDS) that combines Distributed Differential Privacy (DDP), latent space representation (LSR)-based malicious update detection, and blockchain-based decentralized coordination.
  • Targets poisoning attacks and privacy leakage in DFL by detecting malicious model updates in hidden representations and removing reliance on a centralized aggregator via smart-contract-managed aggregation and history tracking.
  • Evaluates the approach on CIC-IDS2018 and Edge-IIoTSet, reporting improvements over existing defenses (e.g., FLARE, FedCC) across various attack scenarios and non-iid data distributions.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Phan The Duy', 'Nghi Hoang Khoa', 'Nguyen Tran Anh Quan', 'Luong Ha Tien', 'Ngo Duc Hoang Son', 'Van-Hau Pham']
  • Tags: ['poisoning attacks', 'federated learning defense', 'intrusion detection systems', 'distributed differential privacy', 'blockchain for security']

item.title: MultiVer: Zero-Shot Multi-Agent Vulnerability Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces MultiVer, a zero-shot multi-agent ensemble (security, correctness, performance, style) using union voting for vulnerability detection.
  • Reports state-of-the-art recall on vulnerability benchmarks: 82.7% on PyVul (exceeding fine-tuned GPT-3.5) and 91.7% on SecurityEval.
  • Notes a precision/recall trade-off (48.8% precision vs 63.9% for fine-tuned baselines; 61.4% F1) and ablation showing the ensemble adds ~17 percentage points recall over a single-agent security analysis.
  • Argues that zero-shot multi-agent ensembles can match or exceed fine-tuned models for security use-cases where false negatives are costlier than false positives.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shreshth Rajan']
  • Tags: ['vulnerability-detection', 'zero-shot', 'multi-agent-ensemble', 'software-security', 'benchmarking']

item.title: Can LLM Safety Be Ensured by Constraining Parameter Regions?
item.description:
Summary

  • Systematically evaluates four methods for identifying "safety regions" in model parameter space at varying granularities (individual weights to whole Transformer layers) across four LLM families and sizes.
  • Uses ten safety identification datasets and additional utility (non-harmful) datasets to refine regions and measures overlap using IoU.
  • Finds only low-to-moderate overlap among identified safety regions and a substantial drop in overlap after refinement with utility datasets, indicating instability and dataset-dependence of identified regions.
  • Concludes that current techniques do not reliably identify stable, dataset-agnostic parameter regions that can be constrained to ensure LLM safety.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zongmin Li', 'Jian Su', 'Farah Benamara', 'Aixin Sun']
  • Tags: ['LLM-safety', 'parameter-space', 'model-editing', 'robustness', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: Agentic Unlearning: When LLM Agent Meets Machine Unlearning
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces 'agentic unlearning' to remove specified information from both model parameters and persistent agent memory in closed-loop LLM agents.
  • Identifies two gaps: parameter-memory backflow (memory reactivates parametric remnants) and lack of unified unlearning across both pathways.
  • Proposes Synchronized Backflow Unlearning (SBU): dependency-closure-based memory pruning and stochastic reference alignment for parametric suppression, integrated via a synchronized dual-update closed-loop protocol.
  • Evaluated on medical QA benchmarks, showing reduced traces of targeted private information across both pathways with limited utility degradation.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bin Wang', 'Fan Wang', 'Pingping Wang', 'Jinyu Cong', 'Yang Yu', 'Yilong Yin', 'Zhongyi Han', 'Benzheng Wei']
  • Tags: ['machine unlearning', 'privacy', 'defense', 'LLM agents', 'data removal']

item.title: Trojans in Artificial Intelligence (TrojAI) Final Report
item.description:
Summary

  • Programmatic synthesis of IARPA's TrojAI initiative: maps the threat of AI Trojans (hidden backdoors) and catalogs their characteristics and risks.
  • Describes and evaluates detection methodologies such as weight analysis and trigger inversion, with comprehensive test/evaluation results on detector performance and sensitivity.
  • Presents mitigation approaches for reducing Trojan risks in deployed models and discusses prevalence of naturally occurring (unintentional) Trojans, open challenges, and research recommendations.

Paper Type

  • Survey

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kristopher W. Reese', 'Taylor Kulp-McDowall', 'Michael Majurski', 'Tim Blattner', 'Derek Juba', 'Peter Bajcsy', 'Antonio Cardone', 'Philippe Dessauw', 'Alden Dima', 'Anthony J. Kearsley', 'Melinda Kleczynski', 'Joel Vasanth', 'Walid Keyrouz', 'Chace Ashcraft', 'Neil Fendley', 'Ted Staley', 'Trevor Stout', 'Josh Carney', 'Greg Canal', 'Will Redman', 'Aurora Schmidt', 'Cameron Hickert', 'William Paul', 'Jared Markowitz', 'Nathan Drenkow', 'David Shriver', 'Marissa Connor', 'Keltin Grimes', 'Marco Christiani', 'Hayden Moore', 'Jordan Widjaja', 'Kasimir Gabert', 'Uma Balakrishnan', 'Satyanadh Gundimada', 'John Jacobellis', 'Sandya Lakkur', 'Vitus Leung', 'Jon Roose', 'Casey Battaglino', 'Farinaz Koushanfar', 'Greg Fields', 'Xihe Gu', 'Yaman Jandali', 'Xinqiao Zhang', 'Akash Vartak', 'Tim Oates', 'Ben Erichson', 'Michael Mahoney', 'Rauf Izmailov', 'Xiangyu Zhang', 'Guangyu Shen', 'Siyuan Cheng', 'Shiqing Ma', 'XiaoFeng Wang', 'Haixu Tang', 'Di Tang', 'Xiaoyi Chen', 'Zihao Wang', 'Rui Zhu', 'Susmit Jha', 'Xiao Lin', 'Manoj Acharya', 'Wenchao Li', 'Chao Chen']
  • Tags: ['backdoor/trojans', 'attack detection', 'trigger inversion', 'model inspection/weight analysis', 'mitigation/defense']
